description_text: Este é um  Banco de Talentos,  destinado ao cadastro de  Pessoas com Deficiência para todas as unidades da John Deere no Brasil . Se você é uma pessoa com deficiência, fique à vontade para se candidatar a qualquer outra posição disponível em nosso site. Caso não encontre nenhuma vaga que seja de seu interesse, cadastre-se aqui, e avaliaremos suas informações para futuras oportunidades que possam surgir. Seja você, seja John Deere. Faça parte do nosso time! Áreas para as quais recrutamos no Brasil Administração Geral; Contábil; Controladoria; Engenharia; Financeiro; Jurídico; Logística; Marketing; Operações; Order Fulfillment; Qualidade; Recursos Humanos; Saúde, Segurança e Meio Ambiente; Suporte ao produto; Suprimentos; Tecnologia da informação. Unidades John Deere Brasil: São Paulo Campinas Indaiatuba Goiás Catalão Rio Grande do Sul Horizontina Montenegro Quem não é uma pessoa com deficiência, pode se inscrever? Este banco de talentos é destinado a todas as pessoas com deficiência, caso não faça parte desse grupo, acesse nossas oportunidades na  página de carreiras . (Por favor, anexe o seu laudo) Por que a John Deere está fazendo um banco de talentos para pessoas com deficiências? Reconhecemos e valorizamos a pluralidade dos nossos times. Por isso, criamos condições para a equidade de oportunidades através de políticas, práticas e atitudes que promovam uma cultura de respeito e o desenvolvimento de talentos. O Que Você Receberá Na John Deere, você conseguirá desenvolver uma carreira que levará você aonde quer chegar. Além de produzirmos máquinas conectadas e inteligentes, com a mais alta qualidade, criamos soluções e serviços que garantam que nossos clientes possam gerir seus negócios de forma sustentável e produtiva. Com conexões tecnológicas, sociais e humanas, você terá liberdade de explorar novos projetos e pensar de forma inovadora.

Trabalhamos para que a vida possa avançar e estamos sempre à procura de pessoas inovadoras que possam contribuir com a nossa missão. Que tal fazer parte do nosso time? Local Principal: Brasil (BR) - São Paulo - Indaiatuba Função: Product Engineering (CA) Título: Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL - Indaiatuba/SP - 118728 Suas Responsabilidades Como Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL, no Centro de Pesquisa e Desenvolvimento da John Deere em Indaiatuba/SP, você atuará no desenvolvimento e melhoria contínua de componentes e sistemas para pulverizadores e plantadeiras, contribuindo diretamente para a evolução tecnológica das soluções agrícolas da John Deere. Principais Responsabilidades: Projetar novos componentes e sistemas ou aprimorar itens existentes para equipamentos agrícolas; Desenvolver e validar soluções envolvendo estruturas, hidráulica, elétrica e conjuntos mecânicos; Criar e atualizar desenhos técnicos, especificações e documentação de engenharia; Conduzir análises técnicas, revisões de design e investigações de falhas; Suportar todo o ciclo de desenvolvimento do produto, desde conceito até validação e produção; Colaborar com equipes multifuncionais (qualidade, manufatura, supply chain, validação etc.); Acompanhar feedback de campo, analisando causas e implementando melhorias; Garantir que as soluções atendam targets de performance, durabilidade, segurança e custo. Habilidades Que Você Precisa Graduação Completa em Engenharia Mecânica, Elétrica, Mecatrônica ou áreas correlatas; Experiência prévia em desenvolvimento de produtos, projetos mecânicos e de sistemas; Conhecimento e experiência com os softwares CREO e Ansys, ou ferramentas similares; Inglês avançado para comunicação com times globais e documentação técnica; Conhecimento em desenvolvimento de sistemas complexos (hidráulicos, elétricos, estruturais); Capacidade de interpretar requisitos, transformar necessidades em soluções e documentar projetos; Boa comunicação, visão colaborativa e habilidade de atuar em times multidisciplinares. O Que Faz Destacar Experiência com máquinas agrícolas, especialmente pulverizadores ou plantadeiras; Vivência com validação, testes e processos de ciclo de vida de produto; Experiência em suporte à manufatura ou a campo; Conhecimento em DFMEA, PFMEA e metodologias de melhoria contínua; Familiaridade com tecnologias de agricultura de precisão: spot spray, taxa variável, section control, JDLink e telemetria; Disponibilidade para viagens (até 25%). Nossos principais benefícios Remuneração variável; Plano de Saúde; Plano Odontológico; Seguro de Vida; Previdência Privada; Benefício Ótica; Reembolso vacina; Universidade Corporativa; TotalPass; Dependendo da unidade, você terá restaurante no local, vale refeição ou alimentação; Auxílio farmácia; Entre outros LI-Hybrid Nosso Processo Seletivo A seleção começa com a sua inscrição, para algumas vagas, existem alguns testes adicionais. Você terá a possibilidade de conhecer pessoas do time nas entrevistas! Nosso processo seletivo é flexível em termos de tempo, então não se preocupe! Nossa equipe de Talent Acquisition está comprometida em proporcionar a melhor experiência para você. Analisamos todas as candidaturas cuidadosamente e fornecemos feedback em todas as etapas do processo. Além disso, todas as orientações serão enviadas por e-mail! Sobre a John Deere A John Deere é líder global no fornecimento de produtos, tecnologia e serviços avançados para clientes que desempenham um papel essencial na agricultura, construção, florestal entre outros setores. Nosso objetivo é atender à crescente necessidade mundial de alimentos, combustível, abrigo e infraestrutura, enquanto promove a sustentabilidade e a preservação do planeta que chamamos de lar. Inovamos para impulsionar a produtividade e proteger nosso planeta e convidamos você a fazer parte dessa história. Trabalharemos juntos para criar, testar, superar para que para que a vida possa avançar. ??

Operate and maintain supplies manufacturing machines and equipment while adhering to all quality and safety standards.Wristband cartridge assembler working on a 4 person production line. Performs packing, wristband, or pre-press functions and operates associated machinery Follows work order instructions and performs labeling requirement Performs quality tests per ISO procedures Complies with all required safety practices and use of PPE Resolves a variety of simple problems of limited complexity by following highly detailed instructions or procedures Receives direct supervision for routine work, new projects or assignments. Very limited requirement for decision making role. Any decisions have limited impact on team effectiveness. Work reviewed for accuracy and overall adequacy Able to work effectively with others and adhere to Zebra Leadership Behaviors. Accepts direction and instructions from supervisor High School or equivalent Ability to follow written and verbal instructions Learns policies and procedures and how to apply them to issues experienced in routine activities

Performs setup, testing and modular level repair to whole units. Maintains accurate record keeping for individual repairs. Uses custom software testing suites to diagnose/validate hardware failure. Recognizes failures, follows engineering changes, and determines appropriate action. Uses equipment such as a voltmeter, computer, and special test fixtures. Maintains production and quality output standards. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Required (safety glasses, gloves, etc.) Zebra provided High School Diploma or GED Experience as a production assembler, having performed assembly operations to required quality levels and job standards Manual dexterity and visual coordination are required. Prior use of various hand tools. Ability to stand and sit on production line throughout the shift. Works well with others. Work for a company that cares, offers a growth environment, and a culture of respect, inclusion and recognition - our front-line workers have an average 7-year tenure! Healthcare, Dental & Vision, Tuition Reimbursement 401k with Company Match Quarterly Cash Incentive Opportunity 20 Days Starting PTO 2 Floating Holidays and Paid Volunteer Time Off Annual Shoe Allowance and free Company Apparel

Working with Account or Channel Management, owns development and execution of technology strategy. Designs solutions that meet customer needs and beat competition. Develops strong relationships with sales team, internal engineering, and key technical customer contacts and leverages to Zebra's advantage; creates and delivers high impact solution presentations. Business and Financial Impact - Responsible for individual revenue attainment with established prices and personal expenses Relative Size and Scope - Moderate to average individual quota size for business and like roles Types of Projects - Many transactions with some moderately complex deals Strategic Impact for Zebra - Medium/mid-term Technical Skills - Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Knowledge of Zebra - Utilizes solid understanding of all Products/Services in business Sales Skills - Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Managerial Skills - Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Business Acumen - Understands and explains how Zebra solutions can help customer's business results; obtains profitable revenue growth via value proposition vs. competition Market/customer Knowledge - Uses full knowledge of customer's business and market economics/trends to position effectively versus competition Engineering degree or equivalent experience preferred 4-5 years of relevant work experience Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Higher level technical skills than typical sales criteria Mastery of the Zebra Portfolio: Mobile Computing: In-depth knowledge of Zebra's mobile computers (e.g., TC and MC series), their differences, use cases, and the Android Enterprise operating system. Scanning and Data Capture: Expertise in barcode technologies (1D/2D), RFID, and how they are applied in different industries. Printing: Understanding of Zebra's printers (desktop, industrial, mobile) and consumables (labels, ribbons). Software and Analytics: Familiarity with Zebra's software ecosystem (Zebra DNA Cloud, Workforce Connect) and how it integrates to provide additional value. Networking: Solid knowledge of Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and cellular networks to ensure devices operate optimally in complex customer environments (warehouses, retail stores, hospitals). Systems Integration: Understanding of how Zebra solutions integrate with key enterprise systems like ERP (SAP, Oracle), WMS (Manhattan, Blue Yonder), and Point of Sale (POS) systems. API knowledge is a plus. These are crucial for being the technical face of the company to the customer. Consultative Selling: The ability to listen to the customer, understand their business problems (not just their technical requirements), and design a solution that solves those problems. Exceptional Communication: Presentations and Demos: The ability to deliver compelling and customized product demonstrations that show how the solution solves the customer's specific problems. Technical Translation: The ability to explain complex technical concepts to both technical audiences (e.g., an IT Director) and non-technical audiences (e.g., an Operations Manager). Problem-Solving: Being creative and analytical to overcome technical objections and find solutions to challenges that arise during the sales cycle. Cross-functional Collaboration: Working smoothly with sales, product, engineering, and post-sales support teams to ensure a consistent customer experience. Self-Management and Proactivity: The ability to manage their own time, prioritize opportunities, and continuously learn about new technologies and products.

Performs the physical and/or administrative tasks involved in the shipping, receiving, storing, distributing and order fulfillment of merchandise, products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. Performs quality checks on outbound and inbound shipments. Performs outbound staging and packing in accordance with site policies. Executes ERP system transactions on outbound and inbound shipments. Updates FMS for parcel and freight shipments. Learns to use handheld RF scanners and Warehouse Management System to fulfill orders. Maintains inventory accuracy. Handles and controls hazardous material on both inbound and outbound. Complies with 5S safety, housekeeping practices, and regulations. Completes all required safety training and follows protocol in all risk situations. Uses Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) appropriate to tasks at hand. Lifts up to 75 pounds unassisted. Certification on powered industrial trucks including work assist vehicle (WAV), off the ground order picker (up to 35' off the ground), reach truck, dock stocker and powered pallet jacks. Creates repair RMA's, manages receiving and shipment issues within repair systems. Develops familiarity with a subset of products, processes, operational metrics and software tools; learns and follows basic work instructions for functional assignments according to productivity and quality standards Provides input geared towards continuous improvement, work instruction documentation, and standardization of processes. Resolves a variety of basic problems of limited complexity under direct supervision and performs rudimentary analysis involving a limited number of factors. Creates expense orders for internal repair consumption. Full knowledge of ISO requirements and demonstrated ability to ensure compliance with external auditors. High School Diploma or GED Basic computer literacy, with knowledge of Microsoft Office. Proficient understanding of the use of a desktop. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Required (safety glasses, steel-toed boots, gloves, etc.) Working from heights such as roofs, ladders, or powered lifts. Moving self in different positions to accomplish tasks in various environments including awkward or tight and confined spaces. Remaining in a stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods. Stooping, kneeling, bending, crouching, reaching, pushing/pulling. Moving about to accomplish tasks or moving from one worksite to another. Adjusting or moving objects up to 75 pounds in all directions. Communicating with others to exchange information. Repeating motions that may include the wrists, hands and/or fingers. Operating motor vehicles, industrial vehicles, or heavy equipment. Assessing the accuracy, neatness and thoroughness of the work assigned. Come join the herd! Work for a company that cares, offers a growth environment, and a culture of respect, inclusion and recognition - our front-line workers have an average 7-year tenure! Healthcare, Dental & Vision, Tuition Reimbursement 401k with Company Match Quarterly Cash Incentive Opportunity 20 Days Starting PTO 2 Floating Holidays and Paid Volunteer Time Off Annual Shoe Allowance and free Company Apparel

Develop and execute winning technology strategies in collaboration with our Inside Channel Account Managers (ICAMs) team. Design and architect creative solutions that directly address the needs of partners and their customers to give us a competitive edge. Build and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with our internal ICAMs and the key technical contacts at the partner accounts they manage. Create and deliver compelling, high-impact solution presentations and product demonstrations that captivate both technical and business audiences. Act as the primary technical expert, solving partner problems and verifying solution compatibility to ensure success. Clearly articulate the business value of Zebra's solutions, showing partners how our technology can drive their business results and fuel profitable growth. A Bachelor's degree in Engineering (or equivalent experience) paired with 2-5 years of relevant work in a technical sales or solutions role. Fluent in English and German is mandatory A strong technical aptitude with the ability to quickly master a diverse portfolio of products and solutions. Proven success in applying solution-selling concepts to understand customer needs and craft tailored proposals. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with a natural ability to explain complex technical ideas in a simple, persuasive way. Sharp business acumen and an understanding of market trends, with the ability to connect our solutions to a customer's bottom line. A proactive and independent mindset, with the judgment to manage complex projects and make sound decisions as a key member of our team.

Designs, develops, modifies and evaluates components and processes used in the generation, manipulation, transmission and storage of electricity. Analyzes equipment to establish operating data and conducts experiments. Establishes requirements for less complex design projects Works on Completing all phases of Electrical engineering design projects Works on analysis of processes and delivers results to necessary stakeholders Analyzes results and recommends solutions Works on developing expected results on a variety of products Develops documentation for new projects and reviews previous results. Works with latest technologies and new approaches Reviews changes or upgrades to existing designs or test Exercises judgment in selecting methods and techniques for obtaining solutions on assignments Electrical Engineering BS degree. Read technical documents and report creation. Make Power Point presentation of findings. Microsoft Office suite skills Communication skills - able to clearly define problem, ideas or solutions. Exposure to C, C++, Python in context of embeded systems. Exposure to RF testing Reasearch Capable 1+ years experience in engineering.

Develops, designs, evaluates, and/or modifies systems consistent with customer and regulatory requirements. Develops system architecture, and ensures the integrity of the architecture/design. Performs system feasibility studies and evaluates alternative technology applications. Collaborates with Product Management and Customers to decompose customer use cases into correct features for new product development. Defines the solutions and system requirements for highly complex system design projects. Represents systems engineering as technical contact on assigned projects. Successfully collaborates and communicates with software and hardware sub-system development teams. Integrates new technologies and solutions based on emerging market/industry technologies. Assimilates technology roadmaps in critical product areas. Lead product feature trade-offs for performance, cost and schedule, while meeting the customer expectations Utilizes, develops, and applies advanced tools, including AI-powered solutions, for requirements analysis, system design optimization, automated validation, and the resolution of complex problems. Evaluates competitor products through data driven competitive benchmarking. Manages and coordinates the activities of other engineering personnel and often performs technical project leadership. Mentors more junior level personnel. Work in the agile development process to release products in a timely fashion Exercises independent judgment in methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside their own area of expertise. Interacts with Product Management to provide technical responses to RFPs and RFIs which lead to successful future engagement with key customers. Will be responsible for supporting lighthouse customer opportunities and accounts via the customer engagement methodology. Bachelor's degree in engineering with 5 + years of experience or Masters degree and 4+ year experience Proven System Engineering experience ideally in the Mobile computing space Able to clearly communicate complex concepts to broadly diverse audiences and guide both local and remote teams in achieving their goals. Experienced in defining system requirements from complex customer use cases. Demonstrated proficiency in dealing with ambiguity and ability to stay focused and to clear barriers. Strong ability to present detailed information at the appropriate level. Experience working on Handheld devices on Android platform. Successfully held a recent role as a System Engineering role working on a product development effort that ultimately led to a successful Market launch/customer delivery. Recent hands-on experience in the full lifecycle (cradle to grave) development of Enterprise class productivity devices and/or the associated application ecosystem is highly desirable. Working knowledge of the Agile development process. Experience in hardware design and implementation. Experience in solutions for Payment/POS applications is a plus. Experience in Android HAL, Framework, Security, and Applications. Recent working knowledge of latest wireless technologies spanning: (i.e.) 4G/LTE/5G NR, GNSS/UWB, Wi-Fi/802.11(x), NFC, HF/UHF-RFID, Bluetooth/BLE.

Communication Skills - Clear and effective verbal and written communication Adaptability and Learning Agility - Willingness to learn quickly, curiosity and need to gain new skills, embrace new tools or methods, and thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. Technical Skills - Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic familiarity with technology solution Integrity - Commitment to ethical behaviour and confidentiality. A proactive problem-solving mindset, creative thinking and a passion for logical puzzles is a plus Basic knowledge of scripting language (for example VBA, Python, JavaScript) or query language (Power Query , SQL) is an advantage Concurrent enrolment in a degree-seeking post-secondary educational institution 0 years

May act as a representative of the CPS team in cross functional projects May generate reports and perform simply analysis / interpretation of system data at the customer, regional or product line level for internal use May contribute to process improvement through identification of opportunity areas and proactive contribution to improvement discussions Engages management as required for assistance in addressing new or complex issues Adheres to OEX customer strategy, leads by example and follows our customer principles Delivers a positive ordering and invoicing experience to our customers and partners Student of economic school, social studies or related Communicative English, any other language (Spanish, French, Italian) is an advantage Data entry skills with attention to detail and quality Beginner level skills in Microsoft business applications including Word, Excel and Outlook Customer service values and orientation Strong communication skills (listening, providing clear and concise information, using accurate language and grammar)

Develops and executes sales strategy for assigned account(s) or territory with manager's input; develops strong relationships with key customer contacts and leverages to Zebra's advantage; creates and delivers high impact sales presentations and capable of product training; often used for those with strong technical background but limited sales experience. Business and Financial Impact - Responsible for individual revenue attainment with established prices and personal expenses Relative Size and Scope - Moderate to average individual quota size for business and like roles Types of Projects - Many transactions with some moderately complex deals Strategic Impact for Zebra - Medium/mid-term Technical Skills - Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Knowledge of Zebra - Utilizes solid understanding of all Products/Services in business Sales Skills - Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Managerial Skills - Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Business Acumen - Understands and explains how Zebra solutions can help customer's business results; obtains profitable revenue growth via value proposition vs. competition Market/customer Knowledge - Uses full knowledge of customer's business and market economics/trends to position effectively versus competition Bachelors or equivalent experience 2-5 years of applicable work experience Uses moderate domain/solutions knowledge Applies Solution Selling concepts to customer situations; excellent communication and interpersonal skills Understands policies and practices related to role and shares ideas for improvement Solution-Oriented Approach: The ability to propose advanced purchasing models like blanket orders or the Supplies Management Program. Long-Term Relationship Building: Creating "stickiness" through convenience and security, making price a secondary factor. Proactive Thinking: Anticipating customer needs so they never run out of stock, ensuring a recurring and predictable revenue stream. Active Listening: Using conversations about supplies to detect signals of need for new hardware (obsolete printers, manual processes, etc.). Business Acumen: Understanding that their role is to connect the customer to Zebra's entire ecosystem, not just sell their product. Communication with Other Teams: The ability to pass qualified hardware leads to the appropriate sales team. Autonomy and Curiosity: The ability to self-educate using platforms like the Zebra Supplies Knowledge Platform and the Supplies eBook. Internal Collaboration: Knowing when and how to involve the Zebra Quoting Team to expedite complex quotes. Strategic Use of Incentives: Actively proposing the Free Custom Label Trial Fund as a tool to eliminate customer risk and close the sale

Provides audits and inspections on product quality according to Zebra's quality-assurance processes Conducts nonconforming materials investigation and may report to Zebra's authorized vendors as appropriate Escalates and/or engages management as required for assistance in addressing new or complex issues Ability to understand specific technical requirements, delivering output following broad framework Other research or related activities as deemed necessary Acts as the on-site champion for implementing and sustaining global QMS processes Conducts regular internal audits of processes and documentation to ensure they adhere to ISO 9001 standards Training local personnel on quality procedures and acting as the primary quality resource for the site Preparing the site for and acting as the primary point of contact during external ISO 9001 audits 0-1 years of experience Strong process adherence Fluent level of English (written and verbal) as well as local language as applicable Good understanding of Microsoft business applications including Word, Excel and Outlook Ability to work in fast-paced, multi-task environment Solid professional work behaviors (attendance, teamwork, time management) Attention to detail

None None To protect candidates from falling victim to online fraudulent activity involving fake job postings and employment offers, please be aware our recruiters will always connect with you via @zebra.com email accounts. Applications are only accepted through our applicant tracking system and only accept personal identifying information through that system. Our Talent Acquisition team will not ask for you to provide personal identifying information via e-mail or outside of the system. If you are a victim of identity theft contact your local police department. This approach enhances efficiency and promotes fairness in the hiring process. However, every decision regarding interviews and hiring is made by our dedicated team, because we believe people make the best decisions about people. For more on how we use technology in hiring and how we process applicant data, see our Zebra Privacy Policy.

Remote Work: Hybrid Overview: At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer's and partner's needs and solve their challenges. Being a part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are a part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve. You'll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about - locally and globally. Come make an impact every day at Zebra. The Zebra Technologies Internship Program is designed to bridge the gap between academia and the professional world, equipping talented college students for a successful career in business. We provide a holistic experience that immerses you in our organization, allowing you to fully envision your future with Zebra. To position you for success, the program includes targeted training, exposure to diverse business functions, social networking events, a dedicated professional mentor, and development sessions designed to help you discover and sharpen your unique performance edge. In this specific role, you will take ownership of a critical initiative: creating the definitive playbook for onboarding new suppliers and offboarding existing ones. You will be responsible for detailing the complete process map, defining owners and lead times, mapping dependencies, and identifying key milestones and completion criteria. This project offers an unparalleled opportunity to collaborate with cross-functional partners and gain deep, practical insight into Zebra's internal operations. This role is expected to start in May and run through August. This posting is for an existing vacancy at Zebra Technologies. Responsibilities: Discovery & Analysis: Conduct stakeholder interviews to document the current state of supplier management, identifying existing processes, pain points, and critical risk areas. Benchmarking: Leverage market intelligence and industry best practices to benchmark and identify opportunities for improving Zebra's supplier onboarding and offboarding processes. Process Design & Optimization: Design and propose an optimized, end-to-end process flow, incorporating feedback from cross-functional stakeholders to gain final approval. Documentation & Governance: Create detailed process maps and a comprehensive RACI (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) chart to ensure clear ownership and accountability. Communication & Reporting: Establish a regular communication cadence with management, providing clear and concise readouts on project milestones, progress, and dependencies. Professional Development Opportunities Interpersonal & Networking Skills: Build and maintain strong relationships with key business contacts through effective communication and consistent follow-up activities. Project Management: Develop robust planning, organizational, and leadership skills by driving the project initiative from conception to completion. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Enhance team-based work proficiencies by navigating and collaborating with a diverse set of partners across the organization THIS IS A HYBRID INTERNSHIP. THIS INTERN MUST BE ABLE TO WORK ONSITE AT ZEBRA'S MISSISSAUGA, ONTARIO OFFICE AT LEAST 3 DAYS/WEEK. Qualifications: Minimum Qualifications: Current enrollment in a MBA with an anticipated graduation date by May 2027 Must be authorized to work in Canada without requiring sponsorship now or in the future Preferred Qualifications: A proactive and intellectually curious learner with a passion for personal growth. You will be an active member of our team, contributing directly to high-priority projects by: Connecting the Dots: Understand and map the critical processes in our company. Supporting High-Impact Audits: Partner with Senior Auditors on SOX compliance, operational audits and testing key financial and operational controls. Contributing to Strategic Risk Program: Contribute to forward-looking initiatives like business continuity planning and Key Risk Indicator (KRI) analysis. Direct Mentorship: Receive dedicated coaching, feedback, and career guidance from an experienced auditor. A Future-Ready Skill Set: Gain hands-on experience in risk management, SOX compliance, process analysis and cross-functional project coordination. Executive Exposure & A Powerful Network: Build relationships with peers and senior leaders and gain direct insight into the issues that matter at executive level. We value curiosity and a fresh perspective over audit or risk experience and are looking for: An Agile & Proactive Self-Starter: You are a proactive problem-solver, take initiative, work independently and aren't afraid to ask questions. A Curious Critical Thinker: You're the person who always asks "why?" and isn't satisfied with surface-level answers. You enjoy identifying patterns and connecting the dots. A Clear Communicator with a Keen Eye for Detail: You believe getting the details right and explaining them clearly are equally important.
